Name: Luup, Inc.
Tagline: Micro-mobility sharing company
Description:
Luup is a Japanese micro-mobility sharing company.

Luup is now developing a next-generation model of electrically assisted bicycles that people in Japan are familiar with, including providing ports (bicycle parking) throughout towns and cities. What sets us apart from other bicycle-sharing services is that we can install 10 to 20 times more ports into a given public space due to their small size. We have already installed ports in Tokyo’s Shibuya Ward as well as in six other wards in Tokyo. Customers can start ride on Luup's mobilities by unlocking QR code via LUUP app.

Name: Synspective
Tagline: SAR satellite constellation & One-stop Geo-solutions
Description: Synspective Inc., founded in 2018, develops and operates Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellites and provides SAR data and analytics solutions. We aim to develop a constellation of 30 SAR satellites by the late 2020s, allowing us to create a new system that can observe changes anywhere on Earth. With a SAR satellite constellation that enables high-frequency and high-resolution Earth observation, Synspective delivers satellite data and various analytics solutions for disaster response and management, national security, and environmental monitoring.

The platform layer provide the basic master data, transaction data and micro services commonly used in the apps. Through using these apps, stakeholders in supply chains are digitally connected and are able to get rid of analog method of information exchange. Name: Axelspace
Tagline: Axelspace is a satellite hardware and data based solution company
Description:
Axelspace provides satellite imagery data through our web platform AxelGlobe on a monthly subscription basis. Clients indicate their Area of Interest (AOI), the area defined by a square measuring 30km x 30km, and pay every month based on the number of AOIs they contract. In order to lower the introduction hurdle, Axelspace boldly abolished the minimum subscription period, which is generally one year or more in this industry.

Having one satellite on the orbit, the revisit period to each AOI is 14-15 days on average. The monthly subscription price is 1000 USD. With 5 satellites on the orbit lat...
